title: I lost my voter ID
answer: You can still vote if your name is in the voter list. Carry any one alternate photo ID to the polling booth.
steps: Check if your name is on the voter list at nvsp.in
Carry any ONE alternate photo ID to the polling booth
Apply for a duplicate EPIC card using Form 002 on nvsp.in

trust.source: Election Commission of India — Handbook for Presiding Officers

title: Minimum voting age
answer: You can vote in India if you are 18 years or older on the qualifying date (January 1 of the electoral roll revision year).
steps: Ensure your age is 18+ on the qualifying date
Register as a voter through NVSP or local BLO office
Verify your name in the voter list before election day

trust.source: ECI — Representation of the People Act, 1950

title: Citizenship requirement for voting
answer: Only Indian citizens are eligible to vote in Indian elections. Overseas citizens may register as NRI voters.
steps: Confirm your Indian citizenship
Register in your constituency via NVSP
Carry valid identity proof on election day

trust.source: ECI — Representation of the People Act, 1950

title: First-time voter registration
answer: First-time voters must register using Form 6 and verify their details in the voter list before election day.
steps: Apply using Form 6 on NVSP or at the local BLO office
Upload required identity and address proof documents
Check the voter list before election day to confirm registration

trust.source: ECI — Guidelines for New Voter Registration

title: Online voter registration
answer: You can register online through the National Voters Service Portal (NVSP) using Form 6.
steps: Visit the NVSP website at nvsp.in
Select 'New Voter Registration' and fill Form 6
Upload identity proof, address proof, and photograph
Submit application and note the reference number for tracking

title: Offline voter registration
answer: Offline registration can be done by visiting your local Booth Level Officer (BLO) or District Election Office.
steps: Visit your local BLO or District Election Office
Fill Form 6 in person and attach required documents
Submit the form and collect the acknowledgement receipt
Track your application status through the receipt number

title: Documents required for registration
answer: You need one identity proof and one address proof document for voter registration.
steps: Provide one identity proof: Aadhaar, Passport, PAN Card, or Driving License
Provide one address proof: Aadhaar, Utility Bill, Bank Statement, or Ration Card
Upload a recent passport-size photograph

title: Apply for duplicate voter ID
answer: You can request a duplicate voter ID (EPIC) through Form 002 on the NVSP portal.
steps: Login to the NVSP portal at nvsp.in
Navigate to 'Replacement of EPIC' and fill Form 002
Submit the request and track using reference number

title: Change address on voter ID
answer: Address changes on your voter ID require submission of Form 8A through the NVSP portal or your local election office.
steps: Fill Form 8A online at nvsp.in or at the local election office
Upload new address proof document
Submit and track the application using the reference number

title: Voting after relocating to a new city
answer: When you move to a new city, you must register in your new constituency and request deletion from the old one.
steps: Apply for new voter registration (Form 6) in your new constituency
Request deletion of old entry (Form 7) from the previous constituency
Verify your name in the new constituency voter list before election day

trust.source: ECI — Continuous Updation of Electoral Roll Guidelines

title: Name missing from voter list
answer: If your name is missing from the voter list, you must register again using Form 6.
steps: Search the voter list online at electoralsearch.eci.gov.in to confirm
Apply using Form 6 on NVSP with all required documents
Wait for verification and check the updated voter list

title: Correct name on voter ID
answer: Name corrections on your voter ID can be made using Form 8 through the NVSP portal.
steps: Fill Form 8 on nvsp.in with correct details
Upload supporting proof showing correct name
Submit the correction request and track status

title: Find your polling booth
answer: Your assigned polling booth details can be found online through the ECI Electoral Search portal.
steps: Visit electoralsearch.eci.gov.in
Enter your EPIC number or personal details
View your assigned booth name, address, and location on map

title: Can I vote from any polling booth?
answer: No. You can vote only at your assigned polling booth in the constituency where you are registered.
steps: Check your assigned booth at electoralsearch.eci.gov.in
Carry a valid photo ID to the assigned booth
Vote only at your designated booth — other booths will not allow entry

title: Voting hours in India
answer: Voting is generally conducted between 7:00 AM and 6:00 PM, but timings may vary by state and phase.
steps: Check the specific timing for your constituency on the ECI website
Reach your polling booth early to avoid long queues
Carry a valid photo ID — you won't be allowed to vote without one

title: Queue rules on election day
answer: If you are standing in the queue before the official closing time, you are entitled to cast your vote.
steps: Stay in the queue — do not leave even after the announced closing time
Follow the instructions of the polling officers
Cast your vote when your turn comes, regardless of how long the queue takes

title: Mobile phone rules at polling booths
answer: Mobile phones are generally restricted inside polling booths. Keep your phone silent or leave it outside.
steps: Keep your mobile phone on silent or switch it off
Follow the booth-specific rules announced by the Presiding Officer
Do not take photographs or record video inside the polling booth

title: NRI voting in India
answer: NRI (Non-Resident Indian) citizens can vote but must be physically present at their assigned polling booth in India on election day.
steps: Register as an overseas elector using Form 6A on NVSP
Travel to India and be present at your assigned constituency
Carry your passport and vote at the designated polling booth

title: Voting assistance for senior citizens
answer: Senior citizens (80+) and persons with disabilities may receive special assistance at the polling booth, including priority entry and companion support.
steps: Request priority queue access at the polling booth
Ask polling officers for assistance if needed
A companion of your choice can accompany you inside the booth (with officer's permission)

title: Someone voted in my name
answer: If you suspect someone has cast a fraudulent vote in your name, report it immediately to the Presiding Officer at the polling booth.
steps: Inform the Presiding Officer at your polling booth immediately
File a formal complaint — the officer will record a tendered ballot
You can cast a tendered vote which will be kept separately and counted if the fraud is confirmed

trust.source: ECI — Handbook for Presiding Officers, Section on Tendered Votes

title: Duplicate voter registration
answer: Having multiple voter registrations is illegal. You must report and remove the duplicate entry.
steps: Identify which registration you want to keep (based on current address)
File Form 7 on NVSP to delete the extra/old entry
Retain only one valid voter registration record

title: Check voter registration status
answer: You can check if you are registered to vote by searching the electoral roll online.
steps: Visit electoralsearch.eci.gov.in
Search by EPIC number or personal details (name, age, constituency)
If found, note your polling booth details; if not, apply using Form 6
